name: Castillo de Mirabel – circular through the Umbría del Berbechoso - Monfragüe National Park Biosphere (PR-CC 66)
name_original: Castillo de Mirabel – circular por la Umbría del Berbechoso - Biosfera del Parque Nacional de Monfragüe (PR-CC 66)
region_intro: Hiking around Mirabel offers trails through varied terrain, including areas within Monfragüe National Park. The landscape features river gorges, dense Mediterranean forests, and rocky outcrops. Hikers can explore paths that lead to historical sites and provide views of the surrounding natural environment. The region's topography includes moderate elevation changes, suitable for a range of hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Mirabel The most popular hiking route is Friar's Gorge Trail, a 6.2 miles (10.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 42 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a total ascent of 146 meters. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castillo de Mirabel – circular through the Umbría del Berbechoso - Monfragüe National Park Biosphere (PR-CC 66), a moderate 9.5 miles (15.3 km) path. This trail offers views within the Monfragüe National Park and includes a significant ascent of 498 meters. Local hikers also love the Collado del Lobo — circular desde Serradilla, a 8.5 miles (13.7 km) trail leading through the natural landscape near Serradilla, often completed in about 4 hours 1 minute. Hiking around Mirabel is defined by river valleys, forested areas, and historical landmarks, with options for different ability levels. The network includes 3 easy routes and 8 moderate routes. The routes in Mirabel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 130 reviews. More than 700 hikers have used komoot to explore Mirabel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find around Mirabel?
A: Mirabel offers a diverse range of hiking trails, primarily through river gorges, dense Mediterranean forests, and rocky outcrops within areas like Monfragüe National Park. The landscape features moderate elevation changes, with options suitable for various experiences. You'll find 3 easy routes and 8 moderate routes in the area.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Mirabel?
A: Yes, Mirabel features several circular hiking routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990746024/castillo-de-mirabel-circular-through-the-umbria-del-berbechoso-monfraguee-national-park-biosphere-pr-cc-66" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Mirabel – circular through the Umbría del Berbechoso - Monfragüe National Park Biosphere (PR-CC 66)&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 15.3 km path offering views within Monfragüe National Park. Another popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1196694917/collado-del-lobo-circular-desde-serradilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado del Lobo — circular desde Serradilla&lt;/a&gt;, a 13.7 km trail through natural landscapes.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while hiking in Mirabel?
A: Hikers in Mirabel can explore several natural monuments and viewpoints. You might encounter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5679306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Gypsy's Leap&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/808762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Cerro Gimio&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5876460" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garganta del Fraile&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4397167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Serrana Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; also offers exceptional panoramas of the region's rugged terrain and river valleys.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or places of interest along the trails?
A: Yes, the Mirabel region is rich in historical landmarks. You can visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/429392" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monfragüe Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which is often part of hiking routes. Other historical sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/655984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galisteo City Walls&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6309687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galisteo Historic Town and Stone Archway&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into the region's past.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Mirabel?
A: The hiking trails around Mirabel are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 130 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the stunning views within Monfragüe National Park, and the opportunity to explore both natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Mirabel offers 3 easy hiking routes that are suitable for beginners or families looking for a less strenuous experience. These trails typically have gentler gradients and shorter distances, allowing for a relaxed exploration of the natural environment.

Q: Can I find trails with water features like waterfalls or rivers?
A: Yes, the region's topography includes river gorges and features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5876460" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garganta del Fraile&lt;/a&gt;, which is a notable waterfall. The trails often follow river valleys, providing opportunities to enjoy water features and the lush vegetation they support.

Q: What is the typical duration for a hike in Mirabel?
A: Hiking durations in Mirabel vary depending on the trail. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1195773619/friar-s-gorge-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Friar's Gorge Trail&lt;/a&gt; typically takes about 2 hours and 42 minutes to complete. Longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990746024/castillo-de-mirabel-circular-through-the-umbria-del-berbechoso-monfraguee-national-park-biosphere-pr-cc-66" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Mirabel circular&lt;/a&gt; can take around 4 hours and 36 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1196694917/collado-del-lobo-circular-desde-serradilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado del Lobo circular&lt;/a&gt; is approximately 4 hours and 1 minute.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the wider landscape?
A: Many trails in Mirabel provide excellent panoramic views. Routes often lead to viewpoints such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/808762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Cerro Gimio&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4397167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Serrana Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. These spots offer extensive vistas of the surrounding natural environment, including river valleys and forested areas.

Q: What is the best way to explore the Monfragüe National Park area within Mirabel?
A: To explore Monfragüe National Park, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990746024/castillo-de-mirabel-circular-through-the-umbria-del-berbechoso-monfraguee-national-park-biosphere-pr-cc-66" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Mirabel – circular through the Umbría del Berbechoso - Monfragüe National Park Biosphere (PR-CC 66)&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 15.3 km trail offers significant elevation changes and takes you directly through the park, providing an immersive experience of its unique ecosystems and views.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in the Mirabel region?
A: The Mirabel region offers a total of 11 hiking trails, catering to different preferences and skill levels. This includes 3 easy routes and 8 moderate routes, ensuring there's a suitable path for most hikers.
tips: The origin of the castle of Mirabel or Peña del Acero dates back to the 15th century. Now it is for the most part in ruins. The views of the surroundings from this point are spectacular.
From this path you have beautiful views of the Boyal de Mirabe meadow and the Santa Catalina mountain range.

The "Padre Santo" cork oak is a unique tree located on the PR-CC 66 route. Its crown is up to 27 meters in diameter and it has taken it some 900 years to reach it. It is located in the Boyal de Mirabel meadow.

waypoints.name: View of the Dehesa Boyal de Mirabe
